title: CaspianDiagnosticRegistry
description: Schema for the Caspian diagnostic registry (`diagnostics/registry.json`). The registry is the single source of truth for the stable `CASPIAN-EXXX` and `CASPIAN-WXXX` diagnostic codes that every conforming Caspian validator emits. The registry is append-only: a retired code is never reused, a new diagnostic for an existing rule receives a new code, and semantic changes to a code's meaning require a new code.

diagnostics.description: The append-only list of diagnostic-code definitions, ordered by first appearance.

DiagnosticEntry.title: DiagnosticEntry
DiagnosticEntry.description: A single diagnostic-code definition with stable identity across spec versions.

code.description: The stable diagnostic identifier in the form `CASPIAN-EXXX` (error) or `CASPIAN-WXXX` (warning), where `XXX` is a zero-padded three-digit number.

severity.description: The diagnostic's severity level. Errors contribute to a non-zero exit code; warnings are informational and do not affect the exit code by default.

rule.description: A short, kebab-case rule name describing the failure mode. Used for grouping and human reference.

message.description: The canonical diagnostic message text. Declarative voice, no trailing period, with field names, byte sequences, and value names wrapped in backticks.

doc.description: The stable documentation URL pointing at the per-code anchor on `caspian.dev/diagnostics`. The anchor uses lowercase `caspian-e` or `caspian-w` followed by the three-digit code number.

examples.message: BOM byte sequence (`EF BB BF`) detected at file start

diagnostics.message: BOM byte sequence (`EF BB BF`) detected at file start
